title: Successful Login with Email Persists Authenticated Session Across Page Refresh
description: Verifies that after a successful email+password login, the authenticated session is durably established — not just a transient UI state — by confirming the user remains logged in after a full page reload and that the session token/cookie is correctly set.
expected_result: PASS: User successfully authenticates, is redirected to /dashboard, and remains authenticated after a full page reload. The session cookie/token is correctly set and the UI reflects the correct identity. FAIL: User is redirected back to /login after reload (session stored only in memory), or the user menu shows a different user's identity, or the dashboard is accessible but the session token is missing from storage.

test_steps.expected: Login page is displayed at /login URL

test_steps.action: Fill in the email address field
Click the Sign In / Submit button
test_steps.expected: User is redirected away from /login to the authenticated landing page (e.g. /dashboard)

test_steps.action: Verify the authenticated user's name or avatar is visible in the UI
test_steps.expected: User menu or greeting element showing 'margaret.holloway' or 'Margaret Holloway' is visible

test_steps.action: Perform a hard page reload to test session persistence
test_steps.expected: Page reloads and user remains on /dashboard without being redirected to /login

test_steps.action: Confirm user menu is still visible after reload, confirming session was persisted
test_steps.expected: User menu remains visible — session was durably stored, not just held in React state
